name: api-documentation-generation
description: Validate that the skill produces comprehensive API documentation with endpoint descriptions, authentication details, request/response examples, and error catalogs.
input: Write API documentation for a user management service with these endpoints: POST /api/users (create user), GET /api/users/:id (get user by ID), PATCH /api/users/:id (update user), DELETE /api/users/:id (delete user), POST /api/users/login (authenticate). The API uses JWT bearer tokens for authentication. Include request/response examples, error codes, and rate limiting information.

assertions.description: Authentication documentation must specify Bearer token format because API consumers need to know the exact authorization header format to make authenticated requests.
At least one working curl example must be provided for each endpoint because copy-pasteable examples are the fastest path to API consumer success.
Error documentation must include HTTP 400 (Bad Request) responses with descriptions because validation errors are the most common API consumer mistake.
Error documentation must include HTTP 401 (Unauthorized) responses because authentication failures need clear documentation for debugging.
Request and response content types must be specified explicitly because content type mismatches are a frequent source of integration errors.

name: runbook-writing
description: Validate that the skill produces an operational runbook with numbered steps, prerequisites, expected outcomes, escalation paths, and rollback procedures.
input: Write an operational runbook for performing a zero-downtime PostgreSQL major version upgrade (e.g., 14 to 16) on a production system running on AWS RDS. The system serves a web application with approximately 1000 requests per second. Include prerequisites, step-by-step procedure, verification steps, rollback plan, and escalation contacts.

assertions.description: The runbook must include a Prerequisites section listing required access, tools, and conditions to verify before starting because missing prerequisites discovered mid-procedure cause failures.
The runbook must include a Rollback section with explicit steps to revert changes because production procedures without rollback plans turn failures into outages.
The procedure must include creating a database snapshot or backup before the upgrade because data safety is the non-negotiable first step of any destructive operation.
The runbook must include a Verification section with specific checks to confirm the upgrade succeeded because procedures without verification steps leave ambiguous outcomes.
The runbook must include escalation information (contacts, conditions for escalation) because on-call engineers need to know when and who to contact if the procedure fails.
